title: Notobuxus benguellensis
intro: Notobuxus benguellensis, commonly known as Benguella boxwood, is a small, evergreen shrub native to southern Africa, belonging to the family Notobuxaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. Notobuxus benguellensis contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves can be used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules, making it a valuable herbal remedy in African folk medicine.
taxonomy: Notobuxus benguellensis is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Burseraceae.
